mood: A spiral-bound practice notebook open on a music stand. The desk shows through as a warm gray-taupe ground; the two leaves are cream 'practice-page' paper carried by --paper/--paper-2. Two signatures define this look and must be reproduced structurally: (1) a spiral-ring BINDING running down the centre spine — render .book-spine::before as a vertical repeating-linear-gradient of dark/light rings masked to little punch-holes, over a soft shadow gutter; (2) faint horizontal RULED LINES on the leaves — a repeating-linear-gradient of ~1px hairlines every ~34px in rgba(70,60,45,.045) laid over the cream paper of .book-viewport (rule the paper, never the desk). Accents are red-pen (--accent) with hairline --edge/--rule borders on cards and tabs. Clean practical sans headings, monospaced for tabs/code. The overall feel: a well-worn, legible reference workbook you keep open while you practice — utilitarian, warm, never ornate.
name: Binder
tagline: Spiral-bound practice notebook — cream ruled leaves, red-pen accent
